title: Verifier Claim Registry - v0
description: Stable definitions for verifier claim names and required evidence. This artifact defines claims; it does not declare implementation support in any verifier build.

claims.name: export.integrity.v1
claims.assertion: Export bytes match manifest `content_hash`; manifest signature is valid for the trusted export-signing key at signing time.
claims.required_evidence: Export bytes, manifest, public key or key manifest/trust root, signature metadata.

claims.name: export.scope_identity.v1
claims.assertion: Manifest identity fields describe the signed artifact: export_id, project_id, export_type, format, compressed, record_count.

claims.name: governance_chain.local_continuity.v1
claims.assertion: Supplied chain entries are locally untampered for declared chain scope: record hash, prev_hash continuity, sequence order, duplicate detection, known chain format.
claims.required_evidence: ChainEntry list or governance_events records with chain fields.

claims.name: permit_chain.delegation_denied_correctly.v1
claims.assertion: `permit.delegated_denied` was correctly denied because the requested child authority envelope exceeded, or lacked, the parent authority envelope under pinned authority-envelope.v0 semantics, with a strict supplied governance-chain prefix and audit.integrity_digest payload coverage.
claims.required_evidence: A `permit.delegated_denied` governance event, strict chain fields from genesis through the denied event and its covering `audit.integrity_digest`, pinned governance record-hash and governance-event integrity-digest semantics, pinned authority-envelope.v0 comparator semantics, parent and child authority envelope payload fields, failed_fields, reason_code, and optional event_id when multiple denied events are supplied.

claims.name: closure.signature.v1
claims.assertion: `permit.closed` closure payload canonical hash and Ed25519 binding signature are valid.
claims.required_evidence: Closure event payload, canonical hash, signature, permit-binding trust root.

claims.name: closure.digest_consistency.v1
claims.assertion: Closure provider/client response digests match catalog-approved evidence events.
claims.required_evidence: Closure event plus provider.response.received/client.response.delivered digest events, or execution.completed carrying the corresponding provider_response_digest_v1 and client_response_digest_v1 fields.

claims.name: closure.dispatch_binding.v1
claims.required_evidence: Closure event plus permit record/export record carrying `binding_request_hash`.

claims.name: workflow.declaration_signature.v1
claims.assertion: Workflow declaration canonical hash and signature are valid for the permit-binding key.
claims.required_evidence: Workflow declaration record, canonical hash, signature, signing time, trust root.

claims.name: workflow.amendment_signature.v1
claims.assertion: Workflow amendment canonical hash and signature are valid for the permit-binding key.
claims.required_evidence: Workflow amendment record, canonical hash, signature, signing time, trust root.

claims.name: workflow.effective_intent_hash.v1
claims.assertion: Declaration effective intent hash equals the hash of declaration intent plus ordered amendments under the pinned workflow canonicalization profile.
claims.required_evidence: Declaration records, amendment records, canonical workflow JSON profile.

claims.name: workflow.permit_snapshot.v1
claims.assertion: Permit/MCP workflow snapshot is consistent with workflow declaration/amendment history at decision time.

claims.name: incident.bundle_manifest.v1
claims.assertion: Incident evidence ZIP contains the catalog-required file set and schemas for manifest v2.
claims.required_evidence: Signed ZIP, manifest v2 file list.

claims.name: checkpoint.composite_hash.v1
claims.assertion: Checkpoint composite_hash equals deterministic hash of sorted chain_heads.

claims.name: checkpoint.signature.v1
claims.assertion: Checkpoint signature is valid for trusted integrity-checkpoint key at computed_at.
claims.required_evidence: Checkpoint JSON, signature, public key/key_id, trust root.

claims.name: checkpoint.tsa_imprint.v1
claims.assertion: TSA receipt message imprint equals checkpoint composite hash.

claims.name: workflow_evidence.sibling_integrity.v1
claims.assertion: Vanta workflow evidence sibling bytes match sibling descriptor content_hash and signature.
claims.required_evidence: Main manifest sibling descriptor, workflow evidence file, trust root.

claims.name: checkpoint.scope_state.v1
claims.assertion: A checkpoint_scope_state sidecar is signed by a trusted scope-state or checkpoint-class key, references an anchored checkpoint whose chain_heads contain the declared chain_scope, uses a supported predicate grammar, and names an allowlisted commitment profile.
claims.required_evidence: checkpoint_scope_state sidecar JSON, referenced checkpoint JSON, trust-root/key manifest, pinned sidecar-format semantics, pinned scope-state Merkle commitment profile.

claims.name: export.scope_faithfulness.v1
claims.assertion: A signed export pack is a scope-faithful slice of the recorded chain for the declared population or declared sample up to the declared checkpoint boundary, and supports auditor testing of the completeness assertion for the declared evidence population and the accuracy assertion for records inside the declared scope.
claims.required_evidence: export.integrity.v1 evidence; signed export-payload scope_faithfulness block; checkpoint.scope_state.v1 sidecar evidence; referenced checkpoint JSON; chain entries separated into disclosure records and proof bridge records; pinned governance-chain, export scope-faithfulness, and scope-state Merkle semantics.
